thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance due = false. Days since last checkpoint = 1; minimum cadence = 7 days. No emergency kill-switch breach across any of the 8 holdings justifies an override. Portfolio weighted momentum composite ~34% vs SPY +3.57% — +30pp alpha spread is the cleanest in the 7-day lookback window.

FACTOR EDGE: All 8 holdings remain above MA200 with positive momentum composites. The highest-conviction holding, KLAC (mom=+45.5%), recovered above its MA50 this session — a positive thesis confirmation after 5+ sessions below. SLV (mom=+55.4%) has the widest MA50 gap (-12.5%) but retains the strongest 6m/12m momentum in the universe; structural precious metals thesis (tariff uncertainty, stagflation hedge, industrial demand) remains intact above MA200. Next rebalance window on 2026-03-25 will evaluate SLV sizing and GLD/SLV relative weighting given the near-term pullback.

VARIANT VIEW: The variant view that justifies holding unchanged rather than trimming SLV early is: the MA50 breach in SLV is occurring during a broad commodity consolidation (GLD also below MA50) consistent with a macro re-pricing event (e.g., tariff escalation reducing growth/inflation expectations simultaneously). If this is a consolidation rather than a trend reversal, trimming SLV now would sacrifice a position with 12m=+122.7% and structural momentum still the highest in the universe. The bear case is that SLV's dd63=-37.15% deepens toward MA200 ($51.28), requiring a larger exit at worse levels — this risk is monitored but not yet actionable without a rebalance mandate.
risks: MACRO: Continued broad equity distribution (SPY 1m=-3.62%, IWM 1m=-6.64%) with TLT below MA50 signals stagflation/rate-pressure as the dominant macro regime. Any escalation in tariff policy (e.g., reciprocal tariffs on semiconductors) would hit KLAC and TSM hardest. GLD and SLV could face further selling if commodity risk-off extends (dollar strength, reduced global growth expectations).

IDIOSYNCRATIC: SLV vol20=60.9% means a 1-sigma daily move is ~3.8% — position could gap further below MA50 on any industrial demand shock. TSM faces Taiwan strait escalation risk with 1m=-6.0% already pricing in some geopolitical premium. GOOGL DOJ antitrust ruling timeline is uncertain — any adverse ruling would trigger rapid de-rating.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: No execution actions today. All 8 holdings are highly liquid. Monitoring checkpoints: (1) SLV close vs MA200 $51.28 — emergency exit if breached; (2) GLD recovery above MA50 $456.37 within 14 days; (3) KLAC close above MA50 $1,465 — confirmed this session (+1.2%); (4) TSM 1m momentum must improve from -6.0% toward 0% by 2026-03-25 rebalance.

trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case: Do nothing. This is a non-rebalance day (day 1 of 7-day minimum cadence). No holding has crossed an emergency kill-switch threshold. The portfolio's +30pp alpha spread over SPY is the strongest signal that current positioning is working in the mixed/defensive regime. Bull case: GLD and SLV consolidation is temporary (macro re-pricing), KLAC's MA50 recovery accelerates, and the fund captures the next leg of the commodity + quality-tech + defense rotation. Bear case: SLV and GLD both trend lower toward MA200 levels, tariff escalation hits TSM and KLAC, and the fund underperforms a defensive cash-heavy position. Bear case probability assessed at ~25% — structural momentum signals across 6m/12m windows are too strong to abandon without a formal rebalance evaluation.
